topic: The Molecular Basis of Genetic Information
title: Genetic Information in DNA

lesson.learn: DNA is a linear polymer composed of four nucleotide bases - adenine (A), guanine (G), cytosine (C), and thymine (T) - linked by phosphodiester bonds. The arrangement of these bases in a specific sequence, known as a nucleotide sequence, contains the genetic instructions used in the development and function of all living organisms.
lesson.simpler: DNA is a molecule composed of long chains of nucleotide bases. The sequence of these bases determines the genetic information that's encoded within our DNA. This information is used by our cells to create proteins, which perform various functions necessary for life.
lesson.keyTakeaway: The nucleotide sequence of DNA serves as a template for protein synthesis, allowing our cells to create unique proteins that enable us to adapt to our environment. The unique combination of nucleotides in our DNA determines the genetic traits we inherit from our parents.
lesson.deeperDive: The double helix structure of DNA was first visualized by James Watson and Francis Crick in 1953 using X-ray crystallography data from Rosalind Franklin and Maurice Wilkins. The sequence of nucleotide bases is read from one end to the other, with each gene containing thousands or even millions of base pairs. This information is used by enzymes called polymerases to replicate DNA during cell division. Recent studies have also shown that epigenetic modifications, such as DNA methylation and histone modification, can influence how genes are expressed without altering their underlying DNA code.
lesson.funFact: The human genome is composed of approximately 3.2 billion base pairs, with around 20,000-25,000 protein-coding genes. The first human genome sequencing project was initiated in 1990 and was completed in 2003 by the Human Genome Project.

people: * James Watson - Co-discoverer of DNA structure and Nobel laureate
* Francis Crick - Co-discoverer of DNA structure and Nobel laureate
* Rosalind Franklin - Contributed to the discovery of DNA's double helix structure using X-ray crystallography
* Max Perutz - Contributed to the understanding of protein structures using X-ray crystallography
* Jennifer Doudna - Developed the CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing tool
places: * Cavendish Laboratory, Cambridge University - Where Watson and Crick developed their model of DNA
* King's College London - Where Rosalind Franklin worked on X-ray diffraction images
* Oak Ridge National Laboratory - A leading research center for genetic engineering
* Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory - A premier research institution for molecular biology
* Salk Institute for Biological Studies - A world-renowned research center for genetics and cancer
years: * 1953 - Watson and Crick's publication of the DNA structure model
* 1977 - First cloning of a mammalian gene (insulin)
* 1990 - Initiation of the Human Genome Project
* 2003 - Completion of the draft human genome sequence
